description.en: A string mnemonic designating the reason this error occurred, for example CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED. The range of possible values depends on the OpenSSL version. Added in version 3.3.

description.en: Mix the given bytes into the SSL pseudo-random number generator. The parameter entropy (a float) is a lower bound on the entropy contained in string (so you can always use 0.0). See RFC 1750 for more information on sources of entropy. Changed in version 3.5: Writable bytes-like object is now accepted.

description.en: Return num cryptographically strong pseudo-random bytes. Raises an SSLError if the PRNG has not been seeded with enough data or if the operation is not supported by the current RAND method. RAND_status() can be used to check the status of the PRNG and RAND_add() can be used to seed the PRNG. For almost all applications os.urandom() is preferable. Read the Wikipedia article, Cryptographically secure pseudorandom number generator (CSPRNG), to get the requirements of a cryptographically strong g...

description.en: Return True if the SSL pseudo-random number generator has been seeded with ‘enough’ randomness, and False otherwise. You can use ssl.RAND_egd() and ssl.RAND_add() to increase the randomness of the pseudo-random number generator.

description.en: Read up to len bytes of data from the SSL socket and return the result as a bytes instance. If buffer is specified, then read into the buffer instead, and return the number of bytes read. Raise SSLWantReadError or SSLWantWriteError if the socket is non-blocking and the read would block. As at any time a re-negotiation is possible, a call to read() can also cause write operations. Changed in version 3.5: The socket timeout is no longer reset each time bytes are received or sent. The socket tim...

description.en: Read up to n bytes from the memory buffer. If n is not specified or negative, all bytes are returned.
